heading: Administration of National Preserve
label: 698u–3 Administration of National Preserve

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall administer the Preserve in accordance with sections 698u to 698u–7 of this title, the cooperative agreements described in subsection (f)(1), and the provisions of law generally applicable to units of the National Park System, including the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (, 2 through 4) and the Act of (; et seq.).August 25, 1916August 21, 193516 U.S.C. 1149 Stat. 66616 U.S.C. 4611See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Application of regulations
children.label: b Application of regulations
children.text: With the consent of a private owner of land within the boundaries of the Preserve, the regulations issued by the Secretary concerning the National Park Service that provide for the proper use, management, and protection of persons, property, and natural and cultural resources shall apply to the private land.

children.heading: Facilities
children.label: c Facilities
children.text: For purposes of carrying out the duties of the Secretary under sections 698u to 698u–7 of this title relating to the Preserve, the Secretary may, with the consent of a landowner, directly or by contract, construct, reconstruct, rehabilitate, or develop essential buildings, structures, and related facilities including roads, trails, and other interpretive facilities on real property that is not owned by the Federal Government and is located within the Preserve.

children.heading: Liability of the United States and its officers and employees
children.label: 1 Liability of the United States and its officers and employees
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, the liability of the United States is subject to the terms and conditions of the Federal Tort Claims Act, as amended, et seq., with respect to the claims arising by virtue of the Secretary’s administration of the Preserve pursuant to sections 698u to 698u–7 of this title.28 U.S.C. 2671

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior is authorized, under such terms and conditions as he deems appropriate, to include in any cooperative agreement entered into in accordance with subsection (f)(1) an indemnification provision by which the United States agrees to hold harmless, defend and indemnify the landowner in full from and against any suit, claim, demand or action, liability, judgment, cost or other fee arising out of any claim of personal injury or property damage that occurs in connection with the operation of the Preserve under the agreement: , That indemnification shall not exceed $3 million per claimant per occurrence.Provided however

children.label: B
children.text: The indemnification provision authorized by subparagraph (A) shall not include claims for personal injury or property damage proximately caused by the wanton or willful misconduct of the landowner.

children.heading: Unit of National Park System
children.label: e Unit of National Park System
children.text: The Preserve shall be a unit of the National Park System for all purposes, including the purpose of exercising authority to charge entrance and admission fees under .section 100904 of title 54

children.heading: Agreements
children.label: 1 Agreements
children.text: The Secretary may expend Federal funds for the cooperative management of private property within the Preserve for research, resource management (including pest control and noxious weed control, fire protection, and the restoration of buildings), and visitor protection and use.

children.heading: Donations
children.label: 2 Donations
children.text: The Secretary may accept, retain, and expend donations of funds, property (other than real property), or services from individuals, foundations, corporations, or public entities for the purposes of providing programs, services, facilities, or technical assistance that further the purposes of sections 698u to 698u–7 of this title.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than the end of the third full fiscal year beginning after , the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Resources of the House of Representatives a general management plan for the Preserve.November 12, 1996

children.label: i
children.text: appropriate officials of the Trust; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Advisory Committee; and

children.label: B
children.text: adjacent landowners, appropriate officials of nearby communities, the Kansas Department of Wildlife and Parks, the Kansas Historical Society, and other interested parties.

children.label: A
children.text: Maintaining and enhancing the tallgrass prairie within the boundaries of the Preserve.

children.label: B
children.text: Public access and enjoyment of the property that is consistent with the conservation and proper management of the historical, cultural, and natural resources of the ranch.

children.label: C
children.text: Interpretive and educational programs covering the natural history of the prairie, the cultural history of Native Americans, and the legacy of ranching in the Flint Hills region.

children.label: D
children.text: Provisions requiring the application of applicable State law concerning the maintenance of adequate fences within the boundaries of the Preserve. In any case in which an activity of the National Park Service requires fences that exceed the legal fence standard otherwise applicable to the Preserve, the National Park Service shall pay the additional cost of constructing and maintaining the fences to meet the applicable requirements for that activity.

children.label: E
children.text: Provisions requiring the Secretary to comply with applicable State noxious weed, pesticide, and animal health laws.

children.label: F
children.text: Provisions requiring compliance with applicable State water laws and Federal and State waste disposal laws (including regulations) and any other applicable law.

children.label: G
children.text: Provisions requiring the Secretary to honor each valid existing oil and gas lease for lands within the boundaries of the Preserve (as described in ) that is in effect on .section 698u–2(b) of this titleNovember 12, 1996

children.label: H
children.text: Provisions requiring the Secretary to offer to enter into an agreement with each individual who, as of , holds rights for cattle grazing within the boundaries of the Preserve (as described in ).November 12, 1996section 698u–2(b) of this title

children.heading: Hunting and fishing
children.label: 4 Hunting and fishing
children.text: The Secretary may allow hunting and fishing on Federal lands within the Preserve.

children.heading: Financial analysis
children.label: 5 Financial analysis
children.text: As part of the development of the general management plan, the Secretary shall prepare a financial analysis indicating how the management of the Preserve may be fully supported through fees, private donations, and other forms of non-Federal funding.
